MINI MINUTES: Brittany Rogers, Esq. appeared for plaintiff. The Order to Show Cause is re-set to August 15, 2023 at 10:30am in Department 610.
Judge: Anne-Christine Massullo; Clerk: S. Le; Proceedings not reported. (610)

And (Renewed) Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action Pending Completion Of Arbitration
Off Calendar Jun-21-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Defendant UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'s Mtn to Compel Arbitration And Stay Action Pending Completion Of Arbitration.

Appearances (via Zoom):

BRITTANY ROGERS, ESQ, Law Office of Scott Righthand, (415) 544-0115, for Plaintiff Louise Xie.

YAKOV P. WIEGMANN, Esq., WILSON ELSER MOSKOWITZ EDELMAN & DICKER LLP, (415) 433-0990, for Defendant UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.

Ruling - A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopts the following tentative ruling:

Defendant UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'s renewed motion to compel arbitration is denied. On February 27, 2023, the court issued an order stating that defendant's motion was "denied." (Order Denying Arbitration, 1:26.) The motion was not denied "without prejudice" and therefore, defendant needs to comply with the prerequisites of CCP 1008(b) for this renewed motion. Defendant fails to meet its burden. Defendant fails to demonstrate new facts or diligence. (Even Zohar Construction & Remodeling, Inc. v. Bellaire Townhouses, LLC (2015) 61 Cal.4th 830, 839 ["Courts have construed section 1008 to require a party filing an application for reconsideration or a renewed application to show diligence with a satisfactory explanation for not having presented the new or different information earlier."].) Defendant's key "new fact" is that plaintiff purportedly assented to defendant's terms of use and the arbitration clause on February 1, 2023. The court, however, held the prior hearing on the motion to compel arbitration on February 21, 2023, and defendant never raised the alleged new agreement nor requested a continuance to file a supplemental brief to address these new developments. Defendant was not diligent and does not satisfactorily explain why the claimed assent on February 1, 2023, was not raised at the prior hearing. Defendant also notes that plaintiff has continued to use defendant's app into March of 2023. The court fails to see how plaintiff's continued use is a relevant fact that warrants reconsideration. The issue is whether plaintiff assented to defendant's terms of use and the arbitration agreement. The request for sanctions is denied.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order.

Judge: Richard B, Ulmer Jr., Clerk: R. Woods, Court Reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR: 13293, T: (415) 274-9977, E: sheliapham28@gmail.com (302/RBU)
